Details:
Data are suggestive that ultraviolet (UV) irradiation is somewhat effective for the removal of vinclozolin. Based on data from one study, 10 and 11 percent photodegradation were observed with the addition of low concentrations of humic and fulvic acid, respectively. Also, the addition of Fe2O3 resulted in 60 percent removal of vinclozolin, while adding the photocatalyst TiO2 gave a 93 percent removal of the contaminant.
April 2009
